claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First question: **Do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you assess eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start with some basic questions:

**How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 7, Blake is 12, and Charlie is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So Alex is 7, Blake is 12, and Charlie is 10 - all under 16.

Next question: **Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They all live with me, but I don't actually live in the UK - I live abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since you live abroad, this affects eligibility.

**Which country do you currently live in?** 

(Child Benefit may still be available if you live in certain countries, or if you're a Crown servant working abroad.)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Living in Spain can affect Child Benefit eligibility, but there may still be possibilities depending on your circumstances.

**Are you or your partner currently working as a Crown servant (for example, in the UK diplomatic service or armed forces)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us works as a Crown servant.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

**Do you or your partner have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you EEA/Swiss nationals with a right to reside in the UK?**

(This can affect whether you're eligible for Child Benefit while living in Spain.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, we don't have settled or pre-settled status, and we're not EEA or Swiss nationals with a right to reside in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess the eligibility for your three children based on what you've told me.

Based on the Child Benefit rules, one of the key requirements is that you normally must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. While there are exceptions for people living abroad - such as Crown servants or those in certain countries with specific circumstances (like having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme or being EEA/Swiss nationals with right to reside) - you've confirmed that none of these exceptions apply to your situation.

Since you live in Spain and don't fall under any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad, I'm afraid you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for any of your three children at this time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Summary:**
Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie because you live in Spain and don't meet any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim while living abroad.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you would likely be eligible to claim Child Benefit at that time for all three children, as they are all under 16 and living with you.

However, the claimant lives in Spain and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not an EEA or Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK. Under Child Benefit rules, you must normally live in the UK to qualify, and none of the exceptions for living abroad apply to this family's circumstances. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 12 years old and lives with the claimant, which would normally meet the age and responsibility requirements. However, the claimant lives in Spain and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not an EEA or Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK. Under Child Benefit rules, you must normally live in the UK to qualify, and none of the exceptions for living abroad apply to this family's circumstances. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 10 years old and lives with the claimant, which would normally meet the age and responsibility requirements. However, the claimant lives in Spain and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not an EEA or Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK. Under Child Benefit rules, you must normally live in the UK to qualify, and none of the exceptions for living abroad apply to this family's circumstances. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children (Alex age 7, Blake age 12, and Charlie age 10) who all live with them. While the children meet the age requirements (all under 16) and the claimant is responsible for them, the family lives in Spain. Child Benefit normally requires the claimant to live in the UK. The exceptions for living abroad (being a Crown servant, having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside in the UK) do not apply to this family. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for any of the three children.
